sourceMovie.title: Sin City
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Sin City (2005) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Road to Perdition
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sin City clicked for you, Road to Perdition (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sin City?
faq.answer: Try Road to Perdition, Layer Cake, Repo Men—each shares DNA with Sin City while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sin City?
faq.answer: Sin City (2005) is best described as Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sin City part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Sin City Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
